exact_text: Built an evaluated signal platform over real openFDA data, combining statistical, machine-learning, and retrieval signals to prioritize high-value adverse-event reports before LLM reasoning, reducing unnecessary model calls while preserving decision quality.

phrases.phrase: evaluated signal platform over real openFDA
phrases.proof: 5 signals on 300 real openFDA reports, each with an honest metric; bullet5_signal_proof.json
router routes 225/300 to LLM by signal priority

MEASURED cost-quality tradeoff (curve in proof): at the agreed >=0.95 serious-recall floor the reduction is 0% — on n=300/68%-serious the signals are not strong enough to cut calls without dropping serious reports. Honest tradeoff, not a free-lunch reduction.

serious-recall is HELD at the 0.95 floor by routing 100% at that floor; deeper cuts drop serious recall (curve shows it). 'preserving quality while cutting calls' is NOT achieved here — reframed as a measured tradeoff.

honesty: n=300, 68% serious. The 5 signals + router + tradeoff CURVE are real. classify F1=0.846 (just under a 0.85 brag bar — reported as-is). The router MEASURES cost↔quality; it does NOT cut LLM calls while preserving >=95% serious recall on this data. 'reducing calls while preserving quality' downgraded to YELLOW per honest curve.
verdict: PARTIAL / YELLOW — 5/7 GREEN. Signal platform + 5 evaluated signals + measured cost-quality tradeoff = real. The bullet's 'reducing unnecessary model calls while preserving decision quality' is a measured tradeoff, not an achieved win at a 95% quality floor.
